Good morning, Flavia. Welcome to the event. Could you tell us about your work?
Good morning. It is a pleasure to be here. I am Flavia Ferreira, a consultant at Sebrae-SP.
Wonderful. What does Sebrae do?
Let me give you a concise summary. We help small businesses and protect geographic indications.
And what is a geographic indication, exactly?
It is a certification that protects products from a special region. It gives our products more credibility.
Impressive. I would love to hear about your mission to Italy.
Of course. I am happy to build rapport with our Italian partners and share the value of Brazilian products.
I am Flavia Ferreira, a consultant at Sebrae-SP.
Let me give you a concise summary of our work.
Thank you for your time. It was a pleasure to meet you.

Good morning, everyone. It is a pleasure to be here at this international event. I am Flavia Ferreira, a consultant at Sebrae-SP. Today, I will present our work with small businesses and geographic indications. These certifications protect products from a special region and give them more value. I am also happy to talk about our mission to Italy. Thank you for your time, and I would be happy to answer your questions.

Here is one simple tip to speak with confidence at a professional event. Think about your register. In a casual chat, you can say hi, how is it going. But at a formal event, say: good morning, it is a pleasure to be here. Keep your answers concise, short and clear. Be assertive but polite. And build rapport with a friendly, professional tone. A good register gives you credibility. Good luck at your next event!

First, Flavia opens the event with a formal greeting and introduces herself. Then, she presents Sebrae and the geographic indications in a concise way. Next, she talks about the mission to Italy with credibility. After that, she builds rapport with the guests during the coffee break. Finally, she closes the event and thanks the partners for their time.
